prompt: I'm building a Go agent harness connected to 8 MCP servers with about 120 tools total. My context window is getting eaten up by tool definitions before any real work happens — I'm seeing about 40K tokens just in schema definitions before my first user message. What should I do?

assertions.text: Recommends Code Mode MCP (not sticking with classic tool calling)
Names at least one specific Go sandbox runtime option (goja, wazero, or starlark-go)
Explains the core mechanism: typed API generated from MCP schemas, single execute(code) tool exposed to the model
Explains that large intermediate payloads stay inside the sandbox and only stdout/logs return to the model
Quantifies the token benefit (mentions ~98% reduction, or 150K→2K, or similar concrete figure)
